Kents Battle Wand (Gilded Cage, Blast, Drop)
Wand of Bliss (Fountain of Life, Cure Severe Wound)
30 Armor on all spots as long as MP > 50 + 1T + 1W
The Shard of the Void Mirror

A fragment of a shattered mirror with the power to open portals to other realms, allowing the user to travel great distances. Only usable by wizards. When using, has to make a WP test or otherwise will be teleported to random location. Also the user looses all MP when travelling.
Negates all pasches rolled when casting (1 Charges)
Ask recently deceased people 5 questions (3 uses non recharable)
When swallowed grants 5D10+20 MP but have to roll x1 Pasch table
Candle, Wax (4 hour burn - dozen) - Black Ritual
Hand of Fire, Zone of Hiding, Move Object, Wither vegetation, Breathe Fire, Animate Water
Bind Daemon, Summon Steed, Stop Daemonic Instability, Zone of Daemonic Nullification, Dispel Daemonic Horde, Summon Daemonic Horde, Summon Great Power
Assume Illusionary Appearance, Bewilder Foe, Cloak Activity, Cure Severe Wound, Wither Vegetation, Change Weather, Breathe Underwater
Unveiling the Secrets of Apprentice Magic
Aura of Resistance, Cure Light Injury, Fire Ball, Strength of Combat, Slippery Ground, Steal Mind
Grüner Daumen: Botanik im eigenen Garten
Golden Revelation (Can turn literally every object into pure gold with a touch and a successful MS test. Works up to a size of 50x50x50m)
Alchemical Savior (Can heal allies by infusing their wounds with magical gold. Requires MS per target)
Shimmering Veil (Enemies must roll CL+Int before each other attack because they are plagued by illusions)

rows: Includes vaccuum - Airborne poisons loose effect
Skull of chaos warrior bound with iron circlet
Against group of D6 lesser daemons/servants or single greater - Daemon test against WP, if failed prone 6 rounds
Reduces fire by 2D4 points - Long range missiles loose effect / Short range -10 BS
All creatures subject to stupidity - Int<3 fear test
Take out any fire within range
Move items (<10 Enc) in 12 yards - Move doors in 24 yards
No attack damage from fire based attacks
Leg bone of humanoid creature being broken
T<5 - MR test - I for still standing - WP to not faint for D6 turns
Statues destroyed instantly - >8 yard walls D6 damage (no T)
Individual or group - only non magical projectiles
No magic/attacks/actions during - Can become visible/invisible at will
Piece of iron and drop of water
Non magical items of iron/steel/bronze is destroyed
Small magnet, pinch of dust from wizzard remains / bones of wizards skeleton
Targets failing MR test loose 1 S + T + Enc halfed
12 yards per level / 8 yards wide - can be dispelled at any time

Air Wing of griffon - Earth claws of a basilisk - Fire Heart of dragon - Water Amoeba
Hits 1D6 - Does 1D10W at S8 + 4D6W flamable - Fear test
In table only movable to upper and lower weather
2x24 yards strip / 6 yard radius - 3D6W at S5 - cannot be dispelled - is flamable
1D6 gold coins conjurable per MP invested. Only known coins.
Travel 100y per 6MP. Caster becomes very bright and melts away.
Golden bars erupt from the ground around target. 6y diameter, 8y high. Has ceiling. Bars 1/2" thick, 6" apart. S-20 to bend bars.
Also through sand/mud - Can be dispeled at any time
All building sections in range collapse - Creatures inside D6+3W
Minute glass filled with diamond dust
Magical wand, wrapped around with copper wire
MS to resist - Spellcasters 0 MP + unconcious for 2D10 turns (clerics / druids not) - undead / etheral destroyed - daemons / elementals banished
WP test - unable to move or do anything
Sphere of glass enclosing 3 drops daemon blood
Immun to all spells except dispel magic - No magical creatures can enter or must roll instability - dispelled if caster moves
Full sized pair of bellows made of gold
Cast on character on brink of death to give a second chance at life. M test must be failed by injured to be affected. (-5 per MP)
Vial of dew collected at sunrise on a mountain top and blessed by a druid
1D6W - can go above max W - removes psych effects
Target flees immediately without test (fear). Each morning CL test to resist. Fail = Insanity point
Touched person turns to gold and is unable to do anything but can hear / see everything. Damage is calculated normal with T. When trying be destroyed Spell is dispelled.
Add 2 MP per extra cubic yard. Can materialize everything out of thin air. Complex items need INT test. No living creatures. 8 cubic yards size. Professionals can tell the difference.
Requires a rare material like Warpstone, Void Shards, or Leyline energy to create.
Jake creates a philosopher's stone capable of amplifying spells tenfold or restoring all magical reserves to himself and allies. Its creation is time-intensive and expensive.

Converts an entire area (up to 100m) into molten gold, destroying enemies and structures. The ground turns into a full lake of gold and consumes everything that sits above. The caster can walk on this gold and allies can as well.

sheets.name: Notes
rows: The Cards n' Ropes enjoys a steady flow of visitors, with an average of 50 patrons per day. The club offers popular gambling games such as poker, blackjack, and dice rolling, with a minimum bet of 1 GC per round. In addition, they provide high-quality dinner with beverages and private special services.
- Spend D10+3*100 XP per month for research
All kinds of mid to high value magical items (Value 100-infinite GC)
- Spend D10+5*10k GC per month for research
Magic ingredients for spells, items, rituals..
Jacob works there as the manager. He gets 1k GC per week

Kent's Magic Utils is a mid to high class magical store that sells all the wizzard desires. From Battle Magic Wands to actual enchanted swords or flying boots; the store got it all. Also for the more sophisticated buyer we offer extremely rare and valuable magic relics and artifacts.
Pact with Tuminifin the Great that Jake does
not age when he destroys at least one greater
Developing advanced spells and magical theory specifically in Gold Magic.
Department of Transmutation: Focused on the study of material transformation, turning base metals into gold, enhancing weapons and armors, and understanding the balance of elements in alchemy.
Research and teaching related to transmutation, precious metals, and financial alchemy.
Supporting the Empire by providing advisors, magical defenses, and economic stability.
Training wizards at various levels—from apprentices to full-fledged Gold Wizards.

Department of Wealth and Finance: Specializes in magical economics, enchanting financial ledgers, and helping the Empire’s economy through magical means. This department works closely with banks and merchant guilds to ensure financial prosperity.

Apprentice: Entry-level wizards who are learning the basics of Gold Magic. They study under a master for several years.
Adept: Mid-tier wizards who have mastered basic spells and alchemy but continue to specialize. They can perform smaller magical tasks for the Empire or the College.
Magister: Full-fledged wizards capable of advanced magical feats and teaching apprentices. They serve as teachers, advisors, and sometimes heads of departments.
Archmagister: The highest-ranking wizards, responsible for managing departments, overseeing research, and advising on key decisions for the Empire. Jake holds this rank as the founder.
Department of Defense: Develops wards, enchantments, and defensive spells using Gold Magic to protect imperial assets, especially in times of war or against magical threats.

Department of Advanced Research: An exclusive part of the college where the most promising minds develop new spells and experiment with forbidden or rare arcane knowledge. Overseen directly by Jake or the highest-ranking wizards.
Training Grounds: A practical space where students and wizards can practice combat-focused spells and dueling using the powers of Gold Magic.
Magical Consultation for Noble Houses: Cost: 1,000-5,000 GC per year, depending on service level
Cost: 500 GC per year for tuition
Description: Nobles and wealthy individuals can hire the College for magical consultations related to estate protection, financial alchemy, and rare artifact acquisition.
Description: The program includes both theoretical and practical lessons in Gold Magic, as well as mandatory alchemy training and basic financial alchemy.
Imperial Advisory Services: Cost: 10,000 GC per year (exclusive)
Description: Providing magical and financial advice to the Emperor or the Empire’s top officials, ensuring their protection and prosperity through magical means.
Description: Aimed at students progressing from Apprentice to Adept, focusing on more advanced spells, transmutation, and advisory roles.
Magister Program: Duration: Indefinite (based on performance and research)
Description: Focused on top-level research and spell creation, Magisters are groomed to lead departments and handle complex Imperial affairs.
Description: Wizards of the College can be commissioned to research and develop custom spells, tailored to the needs of wealthy patrons, military commanders, or scholars.
Cost: 5,000-50,000 GC (depending on power and rarity)
Alchemy of Wealth (750 GC per year)
Description: The College offers high-level enchanting services for artifacts, weapons, and armor, turning them into powerful magical items.

Income:
Tuition Fees:

Apprenticeship Program:

Number of apprentices: 150
Tuition per apprentice: 1,200 GC/year → 100 GC/month per apprentice
Total from apprentices: 15,000 GC per month
Adept Mastery Program:

Number of adepts: 50
Tuition per adept: 2,500 GC/year → 208 GC/month per adept
Total from adepts: 10,400 GC per month
Magister Program:

Number of magisters-in-training: 20
Tuition per magister: 3,500 GC/year → 292 GC/month per magister
Total from magisters: 5,840 GC per month
Specialty Courses:

Number of students (specialty courses): 40
Average cost per course: 1,000 GC/year → 83 GC/month per student
Total from specialty courses: 3,320 GC per month
Total from tuition: 34,560 GC per month

Government Contracts and Imperial Advisory:

Imperial Retainer:
New retainer for exclusive advisory services: 20,000 GC per month (significantly increased due to Jake's high status and influence)
Total from contracts: 20,000 GC per month

Magical Item Sales:

With Jake’s expertise and the college’s reputation, magical item sales have become a major income stream:
Approximate income from selling high-tier enchanted items and potions: 15,000 GC per month
Private Patronage and Donations:

Wealthy patrons, noble families, and alumni contributing to research and scholarships:
Average donations: 3,000 GC per month
Consultation Services (Private & Noble Families):

Magical consultation, estate protection, and custom spells for nobility:
Estimated clients: 5-10 per month
Average fee per consultation: 1,000 GC
Total from consultations: 7,500 GC per month (estimate)
Total from other income sources: 25,500 GC per month

Total Monthly Income:
34,560 GC (tuition) + 20,000 GC (Imperial retainer) + 15,000 GC (item sales) + 3,000 GC (donations) + 7,500 GC (consultations) = 80,060 GC per month

Expenses:
Salaries:

Magisters (10 senior staff):

Salary per magister: 750 GC/month
Total for magisters: 7,500 GC per month
Adept Instructors (10 instructors):

Salary per adept instructor: 500 GC/month
Total for adept instructors: 5,000 GC per month
Apprentice Assistants (5 assistants):

Salary per assistant: 200 GC/month
Total for assistants: 1,000 GC per month
Total salary expenses: 13,500 GC per month

Research and Development:

Funding for magical research, spell development, and alchemical experiments:
Materials, rare ingredients, and research tools: 3,000 GC per month
Campus Maintenance:

Upkeep of the college grounds, magical wards, and infrastructure:
Estimated maintenance costs: 2,500 GC per month
Magical Wards and Protection:

Maintaining magical defenses for the College and its treasures:
Cost of wards and protection spells: 2,000 GC per month
Scholarships and Charitable Work:

Offering free or reduced tuition for talented, low-income students:
Estimated scholarships: 500 GC per month
Utilities and Campus Operations:

Cost of food, supplies, magical reagents for the students and staff:
Estimated operational expenses: 3,000 GC per month
Marketing and Recruitment:

Outreach programs to attract new students from across the Empire:
Advertising, events, and recruitment costs: 1,500 GC per month
Total Monthly Expenses:
13,500 GC (salaries) + 3,000 GC (research) + 2,500 GC (maintenance) + 2,000 GC (wards) + 500 GC (scholarships) + 3,000 GC (operations) + 1,500 GC (marketing) = 26,000 GC per month

Net Monthly Income:
80,060 GC (income) – 26,000 GC (expenses) = 54,060 GC net income per month
D6 x 10.000 GC + 40.000 GC Income per month
A high class shop in Altdorf, centrally located.
Instantaneous teleportation to anywhere in the world using a Void Shard. However, there's a 30% chance of being teleported to a random location
2 Magical Assistants: 1,000 GC each = 2,000 GC per month.
Wealthy individuals (merchants, nobles, political figures), adventurers, and officials who need fast, risk-reduced travel.
2 Security Guards: 500 GC each = 1,000 GC per month.
2 Clerks/Receptionists: 300 GC each = 600 GC per month.
Total Staff Salaries: 3,600 GC per month.

Description: Customers can choose any location to be teleported to. However, due to the Void Shard’s unpredictable nature, there’s a 30% chance that the customer will be randomly teleported to an unknown location.
Base Price: 5,000 GC per teleportation (base cost for the convenience, speed, and prestige).
Cost: 2,000 GC per month for magical components and regular enchantment maintenance.
Success Rate: 70% accurate teleportation to the desired location.
Cost: 2,500 GC per month for ongoing research to mitigate risks and ensure safe teleportation.
Description: If the client is teleported to a random location (30% chance), the business guarantees they will be tracked and retrieved within 24 hours using a magical tracker.
Cost: 1,500 GC per month for property maintenance, magical wards, and utilities.
Mechanism: The customer wears a magical tracking charm that activates if they are teleported to the wrong location, alerting Jake and his team to retrieve them within 24 hours.
Cost: 2,000 GC per month for targeted advertisements, exclusive invitations to nobles, and diplomatic events.

Description: For frequent users (nobles, ambassadors, high-level officials), this subscription includes unlimited teleportation services per month, with emergency retrieval included. Also provides priority service and 24/7 availability.

Description: A business plan allowing entire guilds or groups (up to 10 members) to teleport across the Empire. This package could be marketed to merchant guilds, adventuring companies, and diplomatic corps.
Insurance (2,000 GC): Assume 5 clients choose insurance = 10,000 GC
Total for Core Services: 60,000 GC per month
VIP Subscription Plan: Assume 3 clients = 30,000 GC
Cost: 500 GC per extra item (small items) / 1,500 GC for large items.
Description: Customers who need immediate, unbooked teleportation service can pay for priority access.
Description: Allows customers to teleport personal belongings along with them (e.g., weaponry, chests, or magical artifacts).
Priority Teleportation: Assume 5 clients use priority at 1,500 GC = 7,500 GC
Luggage Teleportation: Assume 5 clients use it = 2,500 GC
Mid-to-large with 15-20 private rooms and common areas.
Intricately tiled floors, high arches, marble statues, chandeliers, and magical ambient lighting. Gold and ivory finishes to signify wealth and opulence.
Wealthy individuals, nobles, affluent merchants, high ranking officials
Calming yet grand, with soft lighting, warm aromas, and faint mystical music. Enchanted fountains and pools with floating candles or petals for an added touch of luxury.
Inspired by ancient bathhouses, combined opulent decor with advanced magical comforts to enhance relaxation and luxury.
Real Estate Acquisition: 500,000 GC (prime location for exclusivity).
Main Reception Hall: Ornately decorated, luxurious lounge seating, drinks, and light refreshments. Clients check in here and receive warm towels and scented oils.
Renovation & Luxurious Decor: 150,000 GC (for high-end materials and furnishings).
Central Bathing Pool: A large, opulently tiled bath with heated, scented water, suitable for mingling and relaxation. Enchanted to maintain the perfect temperature and purify the water continuously.
Steam Rooms: Small, intimate rooms with natural herbs, enchanted to release relaxing aromas.
Exclusive Amenities (furniture, imported plants, statues): 30,000 GC.
The most luxurious suite with a private marble bath, enchanted gold accents, and a personal attendant for refreshment and massage services.
Enhanced with healing crystals and a gentle enchantment to speed up physical and mental recovery. Specially infused mineral water for therapeutic effects.
Infused with rare herbs and spices from around the world. Scented steam fills the room, creating a calming, refreshing experience.
Initial Supplies and Staff Recruitment: 20,000 GC (spices, oils, linens, hiring skilled workers)
A small, private pool with dim lighting and silent ambient sounds, perfect for meditation and unwinding. Magical wards ensure soundproofing.
A large room with a variety of mini pools at different temperatures. Clients can enjoy hot and cold immersion pools, guided by an attendant.
Entry to the central bathing pool, steam rooms, and lounge areas. Includes basic oils, towels, and a light refreshment.
Skilled attendants provide therapeutic massages using rare oils and aromatic herbs.
Includes minor enchantments for muscle relaxation, rejuvenation, or enhanced vitality.
A private attendant for personalized assistance, fetching drinks, preparing the bath, or assisting with special requests.
Serves luxury potions for relaxation, energy, vitality, and other enhancements. Custom-made by the College of Alchemy.
Includes unlimited access to all rooms, priority booking, free magic-enhanced massages, and personal attendants.
Average 20 clients per day at 50 GC = 1,000 GC per day.
Initial Supplies and Hiring Costs: 20,000 GC
Average of 15 sessions/day across various rooms:
Reception and Cleaning Staff (4): 300 GC each = 1,200 GC
Oils, herbs, towels, cleaning supplies: 1,500 GC
Utilities (water, heating, magical wards upkeep): 1,500 GC
Average 20 potions sold daily at 100 GC each.
Average 25 members at 5,000 GC per month.
Replenishing exclusive amenities, incidental costs: 500 GC

sheets.name: Jakes House Altdorf
rows: Stone statues that protect the estate in all directions. Activated when intrudor, enemie or attacker detected.

sheets.name: The Arcane Circle
rows: A hidden society in Altdorf, accessible only to the most influential nobles, wealthy merchants, and high-ranking officials. This club serves as a sanctuary for the elite to discuss strategies, form alliances, and gain exclusive access to powerful magical services.
A hidden, magically protected building in the heart of Altdorf, furnished with opulent décor and layered with powerful wards to prevent discovery.
Also has less bigger buildings in Middenheim, Nuln and Kislev.
Admission by invitation only, with strict vetting to ensure members are of significant wealth or influence.
The building’s location is secret, accessible only through a magically concealed entrance in Altdorf’s noble district.
The club is luxuriously furnished with rare materials, gold-trimmed fixtures, and magical lighting that changes to suit the ambiance of each room.
Grand Hall: Main gathering area for events, announcements, and meetings.
Auction Chamber: A prestigious hall where elite artifacts and magical items are auctioned monthly.
Private Meeting Rooms: Small rooms for confidential discussions and alliances, each warded for privacy.
Layers of protective wards guard against magical and physical intrusions, scrying, and espionage.
Vault and Artifact Display Room: Displays some of the most valuable items available for auction, securely protected.
Exclusive artwork, ancient tapestries, and enchanted objects adorn the halls, lending an air of mystery and power.
Lounge and Bar: A luxurious lounge where members can socialize and enjoy rare, magically enhanced drinks.
High-Stakes Game Room: Enchanted gaming tables where members can gamble using gold or rare items.
Access to the club lounge, bar, and social events.
Full access to all areas of the club, including auction chamber and private rooms.
Full access plus priority bidding in auctions.
Council members have advisory roles and participate in decision-making within the club.
Monthly lottery entry with basic prize options.
Monthly lottery entry with high-tier prizes, including rare magical items.
Full access plus the power to dictate certain auction items and lottery prizes.
Limited access to auction items (cannot bid on high-tier items).
Monthly lottery entry with mid-tier prize options.
Exclusive rights to private consultation with Jake or senior members.
Monthly lottery entry with elite-tier prizes, including powerful artifacts.
Access to premium networking events with other high-influence members.
Ability to sponsor and bring in Initiates for recruitment.
25% discount on all purchases and services.
Private room in the club for personal use.
Special invitations to secret gatherings and high-stakes planning sessions.
A monthly auction featuring rare artifacts, tomes, magical items, and unique experiences (e.g., enchanted vacations, rare creatures).
Property Acquisition and Magical Concealment: 1 million GC.
Bidding Tiers: Only Adept rank and above can access higher-tier items, ensuring exclusivity.
Advanced Magical Protections: 500,000 GC (layered wards, anti-scrying spells).
Revenue Goal: 2 million - 5 million GC per auction.
Monthly high-stakes lottery with prizes tailored to each rank. Prizes include enchanted items, rare materials, high-value gems, and special favors from the club.
Real Estate and Initial Construction: 500,000 GC per location.
Security Systems and Magical Wards: 300,000 GC per location.
Revenue Goal: 500,000 - 2 million GC per month, based on ticket sales.
Furnishings and Decor: 200,000 GC per location.
Total Setup Cost per Chapter: 1 million GC
Total Setup Cost for 4 Additional Chapters (Middenheim, Nuln, Talabheim, Kislev): 4 million GC
The club offers exclusive investment opportunities in high-reward magical ventures, such as rare material extraction, artifact production, and alchemical research.
Total Expansion Cost: 6 million GC (including Altdorf HQ and four additional chapters)
Council members can propose and sponsor ventures, sharing in the profits.
Revenue Goal: Profits vary based on venture success, but anticipated returns range from 1 million to 3 million GC per month.
Staff Salaries: 100,000 GC (including security, event hosts, and maintenance).
Exclusive Events and Social Gatherings: 50,000 GC.
A trading service where members can buy, sell, or trade magical resources (e.g., warpstone fragments, enchanted gems) with each other, overseen by the club to ensure fairness.
The club takes a 10% cut of all transactions.
Staff Salaries: 50,000 GC (for a smaller team).
Monthly gatherings where members discuss political, economic, and magical affairs, often leading to collaborative ventures and covert alliances.
Local Events and Member Engagement: 20,000 GC.
High-value information can be traded or purchased during these events, with the club taking a commission on deals.
Total Monthly Cost per Chapter: 290,000 GC
Revised Total Monthly Operating Costs (including Altdorf): 1.86 million GC
The club has an archive of forbidden and ancient knowledge, accessible to members for a fee. This could include rare spell scrolls, lost alchemical formulas, or ancient blueprints.
Access Fees: 5,000 GC for Initiates, 3,000 GC for Adepts, free for Masters and Council members.
Revenue Goal: 100,000 GC per month from Initiates and Adepts.
Total Auction Revenue: 4 million - 9 million GC
Lea White - High priestress of Verena
Total Lottery Revenue: 1.3 million - 4 million GC
Captain Stalward - General of Altdorf Guard
Individual and corporate investment returns from elite clients across the Old World.
Aldric von Hohenburg – Archmage and Advisor to the Imperial Court
Total Revenue from Investments: 1.5 million - 4 million GC
Other Income Streams (Arcane Resource Exchange, Knowledge Archive):
Arcane Resource Exchange: 500,000 GC across all locations.
Helene Amsel – Arcane Scribe and Translator of Ancient Tomes
Erich Wulfram – Wealthy Merchant and Collector of Magical Artifacts
Sophia Riehl – Astrologer and Diviner for the Nobility
Hans Richter – Scholar of the Occult and Summoner
Isolde Weiss – Expert in Magical Engineering and Warding
Lukas Beck – Enchanter specializing in Protective Charms
Greta Falken – Apothecary and Supplier of Rare Ingredients
Total Monthly Revenue: 11.825 million - 22.025 million GC
Wolfgang Lehmann – Blacksmith with Arcane Enhancements
Julius Feuerbach – Master of Ritualistic Magic
Nadine Herbst – Arcane Advisor to Nobles in Nuln
Otto Bauer – Researcher in Alchemical Transmutations
Leona Schwarzkopf – Specializes in Healing and Restoration Spells
Felix Kreutzer – Master of Illusions and Disguises
Mathias Grau – Merchant and Broker of Rare Arcane Goods
Silas Morgenstern – Historian of Ancient Magical Practices

sheets.name: Aether Weaving
rows: Aetherweaving is an elite magical discipline that grants practitioners—known as Weavers—the ability to subtly manipulate the unseen threads of reality. Through finesse and precision, Weavers can alter probabilities, perception, and even the course of fate itself. Unlike other magics that rely on raw power or spectacle, Aetherweaving thrives on subtlety, shaping events in ways that often appear as mere coincidence or luck.
Utility and Manipulation: Prioritizes avoiding combat, influencing outcomes, and achieving goals with minimal confrontation.
Prestige and Rarity: Aetherweaving is a highly prestigious and rare discipline, practiced by fewer than 100 individuals across the Old World.
Safety and Mastery: At higher levels, the magic becomes reliable and precise, though novices risk unintended consequences.
Subtlety and Elegance: Effects are often understated yet immensely powerful, appealing to scholars, nobles, and strategists.

Founder: Jake, a Grandmaster Weaver and renowned Gold Wizard, discovered Aetherweaving during his exploration of the interconnectedness between magic and fate. His groundbreaking research codified its principles, making it a distinct and prestigious discipline.
Core Text: The Codex of Threads, written by Jake, serves as the foundational guide for Aetherweaving, detailing its philosophy, mechanics, and spells.
Establishment: The Sanctum of the Threads, located in Altdorf, is the primary institution for teaching and advancing Aetherweaving.
Humans and Elves: +4D6 Magic Points per level.
Dwarfs and Halflings: +2D4 Magic Points per level.
Effect: Adds +20 to an ally’s roll or upgrades success.
Effect: Briefly removes an ally or object from existence, reinserting them elsewhere.
Effect: Links the destinies of two targets (ally or enemy). Any damage, conditions, or effects that happen to one target are mirrored on the other.
Details: Tugging on threads of fate to ensure success.
Details: Creates a shimmering ripple of energy.
Details: By intertwining their threads of fate, Jake binds their outcomes. Glowing golden threads stretch between the two targets, shimmering faintly as events unfold.
Ingredients: Two golden threads tied together (30 GC).
Effect: Jake and allies become unnoticed unless observers pass an Int test. (-10 on roll per magic level)

Effect: Detect hidden threats/intentions. You may ask the GM one of:
“Who here intends harm?”
“Where is the immediate danger?”
“What is the most likely betrayal in the next hour?”
Effect: Prevents a creature or object from moving.
Details: Creates subtle cognitive distortions in observers.
Details: The threads of motion are halted entirely.
Effect: Target suffers -20 Agility (and -10 Initiative if you use that) as time “thickens” around them.
Effect: Manipulates a target’s decision subtly (Fellowship vs WP).
Effect: Plant a “thread” on a target you can see. You can later (during duration) focus and learn where they are, who they spoke to, wether they lied to you
Details: Guides a target’s decisions unnoticed.
Notes: Subtle surveillance, perfect for political games.
Ingredients: A strand of the target’s hair, thread, or wax seal fragment (consumed)
Effect: A tiny coincidence happens to help you: keys are on the hook, the right paper is on top, a guard looks away, a candle flares at the right second.
Effect: For social situations, the caster appears flawlessly credible and “meant to be here”. Gain +20 Fel on Charm/Gossip/Haggle/Command-type Tests.
Mechanics: Once during duration, you may declare a minor coincidence that gives +10 to a relevant Test OR cancels a small complication (GM veto for major plot breaks).
Notes: Clothes sit perfectly, words land cleanly, people assume status.
Effect: Reverses events up to 1D6 rounds back.
Effect: Choose one target. For the duration, the world “forgets the hook” that attaches memory to them.
Mechanics: Anyone who tries to recall/describe the target must pass WP Test. On failure they can’t remember identifying details; written records blur; official logs misfile.
Details: Causes intense magical strain if overused.
Notes: Not invisibility - it’s erasure of relevance.
Ingredients: Ink made from ash + a blank signet ring (500 GC)

Effect: Fractures the threads of fate, creating multiple potential realities within a 24-yard radius. Enemies must roll against Int-10 per caster level or become disoriented, unable to act for 1D6 rounds as they experience conflicting timelines.
Effect: Declare one “inevitability” for the next scene (very specific, not world-breaking), such as:
“We leave this palace without being recognized.”
Details: Fracturing reality confuses enemies, forcing them to experience all potential outcomes at once. The air around the affected area shimmers as overlapping realities flicker in and out of view.
Mechanics: The GM must make that statement true, but chooses how reality pays the cost: a sacrifice, a complication elsewhere, a debt of fate, or a future consequence hook.
Ingredients: Cracked hourglass filled with silver dust (50 GC).
Effect: You designate a destination you know (within city/region). Reality “conspires” to open a clean route: doors are unlocked, patrols shift, crowds part.
Notes: This is the ultimate Aetherweaving fantasy: not power - authorship.
Mechanics: Party gains +30 to Stealth/Shadowing/Orientation Tests related to the escape. One major obstacle simply isn’t there (GM chooses).
Ingredients: Artifact-grade thread + a written sentence burned to ash (priceless/quest item)
Effect: You pull a “possible version” of yourself from a nearby timeline. It assists you as an echo.
Notes: The signature Master spell for heists and retreats.

Mechanics: For the duration, gain one extra “echo action” per round usable for non-damage Aether effects (help a Test, reposition, disarm, steal an item, block a spell, etc.). Echo cannot deal direct damage.
Ingredients: Map fragment + gold thread (75 GC)
Notes: Visually: faint threads outlining a second Jake.
Ingredients: Mirror shard + silver thread braid (400 GC)
The Axiom Stitch (Rite of a New Rule)
Concept: You don’t change an event - you change a law of reality in a bounded place.
Effect: The Weaver defines one metaphysical axiom that becomes true within a defined boundary (a building, district, palace, fortress, or even a whole city) for a long duration.
“No lie can be spoken here.” (every attempt collapses into silence, choking, or involuntary truth)
“No blade may draw blood here.” (violence misfires, hands slip, weapons dull in the instant)
“No scrying or divination can pierce this place.” (all remote viewing returns false threads)
“All written contracts bind fate.” (breaking them causes immediate visible misfortune and social collapse)
This is not illusion - it’s an enforced rule of causality.
Range: Touch (must anchor the axiom to a physical “keystone”: altar, cornerstone, throne, vault door)
Duration: 1 year + 1 day (or permanently, but the cost becomes horrific)
A “keystone” item of authority (crown seal / cornerstone / temple relic)
A spool of artifact-grade golden thread (quest item)
Dust from a collapsed hourglass that has witnessed an execution (rare)
A written sentence of the axiom, signed in the caster’s blood, then burned
One-time nature: The Weaver can never cast The Axiom Stitch again - the “Prime Thread” in their soul is consumed.
Permanent “Thread Scar”: once per session the GM may introduce a coincidence against you.
Why it’s mindblowing: it turns a location into a myth. People would swear the place is holy, cursed, or blessed - and they’d be right.
True-Name Severance (The Unbinding of an Eternal Thread)
Concept: You permanently cut something’s connection to the world - not banish it “for now”, but erase the link that lets it return.
Effect: Choose one target that has an “anchored thread” in the world:
a named entity whose influence keeps returning
The Weaver severs the anchor. The pact collapses, the binding unravels, and the target loses its ability to remain tied to this reality through that anchor.
The pact is not just broken - it becomes metaphysically “invalid”, like it never had a legal foothold in reality.
The entity may still exist elsewhere, but cannot use that pact-thread again, and all power streams derived from it collapse instantly.
Range: 12 yards (but the anchor object must be present: pact paper, true-name inscription, blood seal, etc.)
Ritual Time: 6 hours (plus a “naming minute” where a single sentence must be spoken perfectly)
The anchor itself (pact paper, true-name, binding relic)
A blade that has never drawn mortal blood (symbolic purity)
A circle drawn in silver dust worth 1,000 GC
One-time nature: The Weaver loses the ability to ever use any spell that manipulates others’ minds or fate directly again. From that moment on, their Aetherweaving becomes “clean utility only”.
Price: Immediate backlash: the caster takes 1D10 Wounds ignoring armor (this is reality snapping back), and for 1 month they suffer nightmares and disadvantage on sleep/meditation-related recovery.
Why it’s mindblowing: this is the spell that ends “recurring villains” permanently and rewrites the metaphysical rules of pacts.

sheets.name: Calc
rows: Hier stehen immer die aktuelle Werte für die Character

sheets.name: List
rows: pierce Armour -> -1 Durability and ignore Armour
pierce Armour -> -1 Durability and ignore Armour; & ignore Toughness
Candle, Tallow (3 hour burn - dozen) - Black Ritual
Candle, Wax (4 hour burn - dozen) - Black Ritual
